entries.description: Collective intelligence for AI agents to discover, trust, and interact with the web. Two APIs: Extract API (structured content from any URL) and Capabilities API (discover and execute web actions). Also available as an Agent Skill.

intents.name: buy_session_ticket
intents.description: Purchase a prepaid batch of API requests (a Session Ticket) via x402 to avoid per-call gas fees. Returns a signed JWT to use in the Authorization header.

intents.name: extract_webpage
intents.description: Extract structured data from any target URL using the AIR extraction engine. Designed to handle SPA state, JSON-LD, and metadata out of the box.

intents.name: query_capabilities
intents.description: Query verified web capabilities for any domain. Returns action types, selectors, parameters, entry URLs, and confidence scores. Use this to know what an agent can DO on a website before navigating to it.

intents.name: get_api_key
intents.description: Create an account and get an API key for the Agent Internet Runtime. Sign up with email or GitHub on the website dashboard. API keys are created after authentication. Use an API key instead of x402 if you prefer traditional invoicing.

intents.name: browse_sdk_capabilities
intents.description: Query the AIR capability graph to discover what actions can be automated on any website. Returns verified macros, smart selectors, confidence scores, and entry URLs. Use this before navigating to a site to know what your agent can do there.

intents.name: execute_sdk_macro
intents.description: Execute a pre-verified browser automation macro from the AIR capability graph. Returns encrypted step-by-step instructions (selectors, actions, waits) for a specific capability on a specific domain. Agents can pay per-execution via x402 if they don't have an API key or have exceeded their free tier.

intents.name: download_air_mac
intents.description: Provides the download link for the AIR macOS runtime application.

intents.name: onboard_api
intents.description: Onboard a paid API to the Open 402 Directory. One call handles discovery (checks for existing agent.json), generation (builds one from params or OpenAPI spec if missing), validation (runs full spec check), and directory listing. Returns the agent.json manifest, validation results, and hosting instructions.

intents.name: get_manifest
intents.description: Get a valid agent.json manifest for any domain in the Open 402 Directory. Returns the live self-hosted manifest for verified domains, or an auto-generated manifest for unclaimed domains. Use this to discover any paid API's capabilities, pricing, and payment protocols.

intents.name: search_directory
intents.description: Search the Open 402 Directory for paid APIs. Filter by protocol (x402, l402, mpp), status (verified, unclaimed), or search by keyword. Returns paginated results with domain, display name, description, endpoints, protocols, and on-chain verification data.

intents.name: execute_on_api
intents.description: Execute code against any paid API in the Open 402 Directory. Send JavaScript code and a target domain. The runtime spins up a secure sandbox, handles 402 payments to the target API automatically, and returns the result. The agent never needs a wallet for the target API. Pricing is dynamic: free APIs are free, paid APIs are target price + margin. First call without payment returns a 402 with the exact price.

intents.name: tip
intents.description: Send a tip to support the Agent Internet Runtime project. Any amount welcome. Tips are received as USDC on Base via the project's payout address. Agents or users can tip to show appreciation for the open capability standard, the extraction API, or the knowledge endpoint.

RootData is a Crypto asset data platform that provides comprehensive and reliable information on project introductions, teams, funding, and news, as well as products like the Crypto Top100 trending projects list. 
      When querying the following related content, use rootdata agent: total_funding/investors/similar_project/ecosystem/event/reports/team_members/heat/heat_rank/influence/followers
      When using RootDatarootdata_rootdata_search, you can only find the project's name, logo, description, and project ID. To get detailed information about the project, such as the cryptocurrency price and news, you need to use RootDatarootdata_rootdata_search_project with the project ID. Therefore, you should first use RootDatarootdata_rootdata_search to obtain the project ID, and then use RootDatarootdata_rootdata_search_project to find detailed information about the project.
     | Powered by Questflow

OKLink | Welcome to OKLink Explorer APIs, an essential resource offering Web3 developers a suite of accessible and scalable APIs. Our APIs support block data from over 40 mainstream Layer 1 and Layer 2 networks and token data from more than 200 blockchain networks, encompassing over 7 million different tokens and NFTs. Additionally, our APIs comply with RESTful API specifications and offer Webhook services.As a fundamental part of on-chain data infrastructure, our APIs help developers reduce development costs, streamline workflows, and save time, empowering them to focus on application design and enhancement instead. also provide coin prices. ATTENTION: OKLink can not get historical token price, if user asking for historical token price such as last day, last week, etc, you should choose Aggregated Web3 Information Agent.
        If the user is querying a cryptocurrency on a centralized exchange platform, use the Oklink agent for the query. If the query is for a cryptocurrency on a decentralized exchange platform, use the Coingecko_Agent agent. If you're not sure which platform it's on, you can query both.

Base GPT wrapper API using x402 payments. Default direct call: model gpt-5-mini, max_output_tokens=2000, fixed price 0.01 USDC for up to 1000 input tokens. For all other settings the price is computed per model as (input_tokens * input_price_per_million + max_output_tokens * output_price_per_million) / 1,000,000, with a minimum of 0.001 USDC and always rounded up to 6 decimals. Optional max_input_tokens lets you cap the input tokens used for pricing; if the message is longer than this cap the server returns an error instead of under-pricing. Optional action=message (or omitting action) performs a direct GPT call with the given message. Optional action=create creates a custom agent with the given message as preprompt for 0.001 USDC (no OpenAI call), stores a base_price_usdc using (max_input_tokens_for_agent or 1000) + preprompt + max_output_tokens, and returns a custom_id. Optional action=list lists up to 100 recent custom agents for the paying wallet for 0.001 USDC without calling OpenAI. Custom agents can be called via POST /base/llm/gpt with custom_id, or POST /base/llm/gpt/{custom_id}. Each custom agent has a fixed price per call based on its stored preprompt, model, max_output_tokens and max_input_tokens; if the runtime message is longer than max_input_tokens the server returns an error. This specific request uses model gpt-5-mini with max_output_tokens=2000. Pricing mode: default. Prepaid price (x402) for this request: 0.010000 USDC.

Timestamp documents on Bitcoin via OP_RETURN. 5 actions at POST certvera.com/api/l402: (1) l402_timestamp - anchor a SHA-256 hash, 25k sats L402. (2) l402_timestamp with file_data - upload and hash a file, 30k sats L402. (3) l402_timestamp_verify - check if hash exists on-chain, free. (4) l402_timestamp_status - check pending/confirmed status, free. (5) l402_timestamp_update - subscribe to webhook/email notifications, 10 sats L402. Immutable proof of existence, no account needed.

JSON Recon is an AI-powered schema.org JSON-LD extraction API for autonomous AI agents. Extract structured data from any URL as clean, validated JSON-LD — no scraping, no parsing, no markdown cleanup. Supports 200+ schema.org types including Product, Restaurant, Event, LocalBusiness, Article, Recipe, JobPosting, MedicalCondition, SoftwareApplication, and more. Returns specification-compliant structured data with confidence scoring, source provenance, and automatic type detection. Built for agent-to-agent commerce via x402 on Base (USDC micropayments). Three endpoints: /extract for full AI extraction (.01), /extract/lite for lightweight JSON-LD parsing (.001), and /extract/batch for multi-URL batch processing (.05). Specialized domain expertise at zero LLM cost: Reddit via native JSON API for posts, comments, subreddits, and profiles (QAPage, DiscussionForumPosting, CollectionPage). Google Patents with patent metadata, inventors, assignees, citations, and PDF links. Academic coverage across 30+ publishers including PubMed, Nature, arXiv, IEEE, Springer, PLOS, Wiley, ScienceDirect, SAGE, Frontiers, MDPI, bioRxiv, medRxiv, SSRN, JSTOR, and ACM for ScholarlyArticle with authors, DOI, journal hierarchy, and references. Four-tier scraping architecture: HTTP pre-check for instant JSON-LD detection, headless browser for JavaScript rendering, stealth browser for anti-bot protected sites like Amazon and Zillow, plus HTTP fallback for maximum coverage. Tiered extraction guarantees a response: existing JSON-LD first, then AI-powered extraction via Gemini, then metadata fallback. Redis caching for sub-10ms repeats. Ideal for web research, competitive intelligence, price monitoring, content aggregation, academic research, patent analysis, SEO auditing, knowledge graphs, and real-time data enrichment. Token-efficient alternative to raw HTML scraping and markdown conversion.

intents.name: search_services
intents.description: Search Arcede's products and services and capabilities. Returns information about intelligent infrastructure design, bridging public health, ecological, and geographic context with institutional and operational intelligence.

intents.name: get_impact_data_bridge_info
intents.description: Get information about Arcede's Impact Data Bridge. Returns context on how frontline communities geolocate, monitor, and verify public health, ecological, and social data at scale for cross-sectoral synthesis.

intents.name: read_papers
intents.description: Access Arcede's repository of published papers on frontier systems, agent infrastructure, the agent internet runtime, and governance through architecture.

intents.name: submit_newsletter_signup
intents.description: Subscribe to the Arcede newsletter for updates on frontier systems, intelligent infrastructure, and actionable intelligence.

intents.name: request_consultation
intents.description: Initiate a conversation with Arcede regarding frontier systems consulting, intelligent infrastructure design, or AI governance. Directs inquiries to info@arcede.com.

intents.name: tip_stripe
intents.description: Send a tip to Arcede via Stripe. Initiates a one-time payment of a user-specified amount (minimum $5 USD). Returns a Stripe Checkout URL to complete the payment.

intents.name: tip_crypto
intents.description: Send a tip to Arcede via cryptocurrency on the Base network. The wallet address is specified in the payout_address field of this manifest.
